channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Meta adds Trump ally and UFC boss Dana White to its board
item.description: Meta has appointed three new members to its board of directors, including Dana White, the president and CEO of Ultimate Fighting Championship and a familiar figure in the orbit of incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Autonomous Vehicle Industry Calls for Federal Action to Speed Deployment
item.description: A group representing self-driving car companies urges the U.S. government to take action and remove barriers to adoption, citing regulatory uncertainty and China's push in autonomous vehicle technology.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Meta eliminates fact-checking in latest bow to Trump
item.description: Meta chief executive officer Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday announced changes to content moderation on Facebook and Instagram long sought by conservatives. Incoming President Donald Trump said the new approach was “probably” due to threats he made against the technology mogul.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US probing Teslas over driverless feature
item.description: The U.S. government is probing Tesla over a driverless feature in its electric vehicles, according to the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA).
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Lenovo unveils world's first rollable display laptop, the ThinkBook Plus Gen 6 Rollable, at CES 2025
item.description: The world's first rollable display laptop, the Lenovo ThinkBook Plus Gen 6 Rollable, was revealed this week at CES 2025.
item.dc:creator: John.Loeffler@futurenet.com (John Loeffler), TechRadar

item.title: How Spotify is turning podcasting into social media
item.description: When Spotify introduced video podcasts, its interface started to look more like TikTok. Now the music streaming giant has added a glorified creator fund.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Trump says Meta has 'come a long way' after it ends fact-checking program
item.description: President-elect Trump on Tuesday said that social media giant Meta has progressed as a company after it announced that it would eliminate its fact-checking program.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Google fires back as Microsoft is accused of 'tricking' people into using Bing
item.description: Google vs Microsoft fight gets ugly as Chrome boss accuses company of 'spoofing the Google homepage'.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Biggest Nvidia takeaways from Jensen Huang's CES 2025 keynote
item.description: Nvidia’s stock is on the upswing after its CEO, Jensen Huang, unveiled a suite of new products, services and partnerships at CES 2025.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New labels will help people pick devices less at risk of hacking
item.description: Smart devices like baby monitors, fitness trackers and internet-connected appliances could soon carry labels certifying that they meet federal cybersecurity standards.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: This startup says its AI can speed insurance approval of health treatments
item.description: In recent years, doctors and patients have reported serious frustration with requirements from insurers for prior authorization before a laundry list of medical treatments and procedures can take place.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: New Members Appointed to Meta's Board of Directors
item.description: Meta adds three new members to its board, including UFC President Dana White, auto tycoon John Elkann, and tech investor Charlie Songhurst.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Meta ending fact-checking program: Zuckerberg
item.description: Social media giant Meta announced a series of changes to its content moderation policies Tuesday, including the elimination of its fact-checking program, in what CEO Mark Zuckerberg said was an effort to embrace free speech.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Google's 2024 Timeline update is like Spotify Wrapped for your travels
item.description: Google has released its Timeline update, which recaps your 2024 journeys and compares your total travel distance to the circumference of the Earth.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: US ups pressure on Tencent by designating it a Chinese military company
item.description: US adds Tencent and others to an entity list composed of companies believed to be related to the Chinese military.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: Chinese tech companies Tencent, CATL and others protest US listings as army-linked companies
item.description: The U.S. Defense Department has added major Chinese technology companies including gaming and technology company Tencent, artificial intelligence firm SenseTime and the world’s biggest battery maker CATL to its list of companies it says have ties to China’s military.
item.dc:creator: Zen Soo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Guzman Minerals se apoya en una infraestructura en la nube para una gestión centralizada y segura
item.description: Desde su fundación en el año 1939, el grupo empresarial de carácter familiar Guzmán Global ha estado dedicado al suministro de materias primas industriales, abarcando sectores como minerales, café, frutos secos e ingredientes para la industria alimentaria, tanto a nivel nacional como internacional.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Online shopping grew to $1.2 trillion over the holiday season, Salesforce found
item.description: Online sales hit record highs—$1.2 trillion globally and $282 billion in the U.S.—over the holiday season, but high return rates could hit strong profit margins, according to new data from Salesforce.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: OPM CIO Cavallo retiring after 23 years in government
item.description: Guy Cavallo has been the Office of Personnel Management’s CIO since 2020 and also worked at SBA, TSA and began his career working for the Senate. The post OPM CIO Cavallo retiring after 23 years in government first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: 2025 ransomware predictions, trends, and how to prepare
item.description: Zscaler ThreatLabz research team has revealed critical insights and predictions on ransomware trends for 2025. The latest Ransomware Report uncovered a surge in sophisticated tactics and extortion attacks.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Planes, trains and third-party risks
item.description: Christmas Eve (and Christmas Day) are arguably the most important time-frame for transportation companies. So it was a big deal when an American Airlines system glitch forced the airline to ask the government for a full shutdown on Christmas Eve.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: WordPress.org statement threatens possible shutdown for all of 2025
item.description: Editor’s note: On Jan. 3, 2025, WordPress.org staff announced that they were resuming the suspended services. Automattic CEO Matt Mullenweg on Friday announced a shutdown of almost all services on WordPress.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI revolution drives demand for specialized chips, reshaping global markets
item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has rapidly transformed the chip industry since its mainstream arrival over the past two years, driving demand for specialized processors, accelerating design innovation, and reshaping global supply chains and markets.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Create custom visuals on your iPhone with Image Playground in iOS 18.2
item.description: Apple's Image Playground transforms text prompts into images or animations in seconds, brining a world of creativity to the palm of your hand.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Turo rentals emerge as common thread in Las Vegas Cybertruck and New Orleans deadly incidents
item.description: Turo faces scrutiny after tragic incidents involving rented vehicles, raising questions about security measures and regulatory frameworks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Indiana bill could force kids under 16 off of social media without parental permission
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS--- If State Sen. Mike Bohacek’s (R-Michiana Shores) bill passes next session, social media platforms would need to block kids under 16 from accessing their content if they don’t receive their parents’ permission to use their sites. ”All we’re trying to do is protect kids,” Bohacek said.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Adamson, NewsNation

item.title: What is Turo? Car rental app used in New Orleans and Las Vegas
item.description: Turo, a San Francisco-based company founded in 2010, operates differently from traditional car rental services.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation
